title: Sip Into Sweet Chocolate Heaven Day
story: Hot chocolate lovers unite every January 31 for National Hot Chocolate Day! People celebrate by making their favorite warm chocolate drinks with marshmallows, whipped cream, or candy cane stirrers. Coffee shops offer special flavors like peppermint mocha or salted caramel hot chocolate. Families gather in kitchens to heat up milk and stir in chocolate powder or melted chocolate bars. Some people add cinnamon, vanilla, or even chili powder for a spicy kick! Social media fills with photos of steaming mugs topped with colorful marshmallows. It's the perfect winter treat to warm up cold January days.
activity_question: What do people commonly add to their hot chocolate on this holiday?
bonus_fact: The Aztecs drank chocolate cold and bitter, very different from today's sweet hot chocolate!

title: Art Makes Hearts Happy Day
story: Every January 31, people celebrate National Inspire Your Heart with Art Day by filling their lives with creativity! Families visit museums, draw pictures together, or make crafts at home. Some people share their artwork on social media or try a new art project they've never done before. Schools might have special art activities, and libraries often host painting or sculpture workshops. The goal is simple: let art make you feel happy, excited, or amazed. Whether you're looking at famous paintings, doodling in a notebook, or building something with clay, this day reminds everyone that art can touch your heart in wonderful ways.
activity_question: What is the main goal of National Inspire Your Heart with Art Day?
activity_choices: To let art make you feel happy, excited, or amazed
To teach people to paint like unicorns
activity_answer: To let art make you feel happy, excited, or amazed
follow_up_question: What kind of art project would inspire your heart the most?

title: National Backward Day Goes Forward!
story: People across the country flip their world upside down every January 31 for National Backward Day! On this silly holiday, kids wear their clothes backwards, walk backwards to school, and even try eating dessert before dinner. Some families write their names backwards on social media posts or spell words in reverse. Teachers might start class by saying goodbye instead of hello. The whole point is to do ordinary things in the opposite way and have a good laugh about it. It's a perfect excuse to be goofy and see how confusing life gets when everything goes the wrong way around!
activity_question: What might people do on National Backward Day?
follow_up_question: What would be the hardest thing for you to do backwards for a whole day?

title: Brussels Sprouts Get Their Big Day!
story: January 31 is National Eat Brussels Sprouts Day, when these tiny green vegetables finally get some love! People share creative recipes online, from roasted sprouts with bacon to cheesy casseroles. Many families challenge picky eaters to try them prepared in new ways, proving these mini cabbages can actually taste amazing.
activity_question: What do people often add to brussels sprouts to make them taste better?
follow_up_question: What's the weirdest way you could imagine preparing brussels sprouts?

title: Fun at Work Day Makes Jobs Awesome
story: Every January 31, offices around the world transform into playgrounds for National Fun at Work Day! Employees wear silly costumes, bring pets to the office, and organize crazy contests like desk decorating competitions. Some companies host pizza parties, game tournaments, or even talent shows during lunch breaks. Bosses might dress up as superheroes while workers play board games in conference rooms. The whole point is making work feel less like work and more like hanging out with friends. Many people share funny workplace photos on social media to spread the joy and show how creative their teams can be.
activity_question: What might employees do to celebrate National Fun at Work Day?
activity_choices: Wear silly costumes and bring pets to work
activity_answer: Wear silly costumes and bring pets to work
follow_up_question: What fun activity would you organize if you were the boss for a day?
